item.title: A really short story
item.content:encoded:
The following communication from GREY WANDERER was received by a Centre dead drop:

You are doing great work. I have spoken with the Ambassador and he is willing to treat with you through the actor so long as you are diplomatic.

The following occured after contact with ONYX POET was restored.

ONYX POET: WHAT ARE YOU EVEN DOING? THE LAST DAEVA SAID HE TOLD YOU GUYS TO CHILL.

ONYX POET: YOU KNOW WHAT? FUCK THIS NOISE.

ONYX POET: ALAGADDA OUT.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: I'm willing to make the call that the original image was public domain given this information. I'll consider this solved.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
The images used from SCP-1981 are not original to SCP-1981 and are therefore non-compliant for reuse without determining the original source.

I'm pretty sure that the images from 1981 are literally just altered screencaps from the Evil Empire speech by Reagan, and recordings of speeches given by federal officials (such as the president) are public domain.

E: Might not be my place, but per the Ronald Reagan Presidential Library, the White House Television collection says this:

White House Television Office (WHTV) videotapes: 1981-89 (6,750 items)
The WHTV film crews provided daily coverage of President Reagan's activities. This collection is arranged chronological and includes oval office visits, speeches, state visits, domestic trips and foreign trips and bill signings. A list of President Reagan's major speeches is available. This collection is in the public domain. The collection is covered by the Presidential Records Act but most tapes are open for research.

In those speeches is, indeed, the Evil Empire speech. So I'm pretty sure the original 1981 image is Kosher, and therefore, so it this.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Snipes, it would benefit you to pay attention and actually read staff posts before responding.

The images used from SCP-1981 are not original to SCP-1981 and are therefore non-compliant for reuse without determining the original source.

item.title: Re: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded: He did in fact source his images, and they do follow CC. I know it's not my place to talk but this license warning seems superfluous.

item.title: Staff Post: Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. The images used from SCP-1981 are not original to SCP-1981 and are therefore non-compliant for reuse without determining the original source.

Edit: See IHP's post below.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A wonderful harvest of fatty fatty fatty seals."
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When that day comes, I'll hopefully be a good enough writer to retool this into a bizarre Kafkaesque horror about sharks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There will be a day when punching sharks is not funny, but it is not this day. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's more than enough for it to be a "real" GOI. We just need a hub.

I will get to that after finals and the IJAMEA business.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Union of American States, actually. So yeah "American" is totally appropriate. Mainly I was just pointing out that we'd been doing this from the beginning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I'm headcanoning UAS as "United American States", which is why I use "American" as the adjective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm hoping that soon enough there'll be enough work for SPC to be a canon.

There's more than enough for it to be a "real" GOI. We just need a hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isn't even the United States. See SPC-140

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Up on the fields they labor away, we'll seize the means of production someday!

Nice

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why, thank you. The alternate universe conceits were heavily encouraged by MrWrong in SPC-2615, so I can't take all the credit for that.

I'm hoping that soon enough there'll be enough work for SPC to be a canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so…

Between this article not noticing Reagan was ever President, Great American Watchmen, and a fucking sharknado in San Francisco, I think this is our confirmation that the SPC lives in a bizarro-universe.

Centre agents were summarily dispatched to Kitty Hawk where they encountered an abnormally high level of Selachian Entities, and they engaged in pugilism.

My god, just the blitheness with which lines like this are thrown out. I'm so proud our little silly joke has come so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
bad post op

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got it. That seems to be the big thing that needs work, and I have a few ideas about widening the world of the SPC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some of the alternate universe stuff was fun but ultimately doesn't go far enough. If you want this to work you really need to go all-in on that aspect of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this on the forums. Liked in then, liked it now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked the Reaganshark pic, but I feel like this doesn't go far enough in its parody - a large part of it still really is just "1981 but with sharks", without really branching into new territory. The last log is funny though, but ultimately it just seems like it doesn't go anywhere from its initial premise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty much just a skip ported to the SPC format. Nah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My very first SPC!

Thanks to MrWrong for his help critiquing the draft, and the good folks in chat who took a look at it.
Inspired by SCP-1981 and all of the recent SPC articles that have been making their way around recently.

Licensing
– hide block

The base image was used in SCP-1981. Thanks to Lazar in #site17 for telling me that it was fair use.
The image of the shark used in photo manipulation was By Hein waschefort (Own work) CC BY-SA 3.0 (http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0), via Wikimedia Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure about this one. On one hand, there were some funny moments in it, but on the other hand, it was more or less just "SCP-1981 but SPC." No vote for me.
